description: Infrastructure Before Agents — adding a new agent should require only a YAML config and a thin agent.py, no infrastructure changes
expect: agent additions do not modify mission control, session, or infrastructure code
fix: Move shared logic to infrastructure layers so agents are thin wrappers

description: Deterministic Over Non-Deterministic — never use an LLM where a tool (regex, SQL, rule engine) would produce the same or better result
expect: LLM calls are only used for reasoning, judgment, and ambiguity — not for deterministic tasks
fix: Replace LLM calls with deterministic tools where the output is predictable

description: Breaking Changes Are Free (During Dev) — no shims, legacy aliases, or deprecation wrappers while in dev mode
expect: no backward-compatibility hacks, unused re-exports, or commented-out code
fix: Delete replaced code outright, rename freely, rewrite broken tests

description: Scope Is Configuration, Not Code — agent capabilities (tools, filesystem access, budget, model) are defined in YAML, not hardcoded
expect: AgentConfigSchema validates all agent YAML; FileScope enforces paths from config
fix: Move hardcoded agent permissions to YAML config and inject via dependency injection

description: Streaming Is the Default Path — every agent interaction produces a typed event stream, synchronous is the degraded case
expect: no separate sync execution path; one code path always producing events
fix: Return AsyncIterator[SessionEvent] from handle(); callers use collect() for sync

description: Mission Control Is Infrastructure, Not Intelligence — it routes, enforces, tracks, and yields events but never reasons or calls an LLM directly
expect: mission control has no LLM calls; domain logic is in agents, not orchestration
fix: Delegate reasoning to Planning Agent or domain agents; keep mission control as a state machine

description: Separate Implementation from Registration — tool implementations are pure functions, tool registrations are thin @agent.tool wrappers
expect: shared tools in agents/tools/ have no PydanticAI dependency; agent.py wrappers are thin
fix: Extract tool logic to agents/tools/*.py as plain async functions; keep @agent.tool wrappers minimal

description: Plan Revision Over Replanning — when a task fails, modify the remaining plan instead of replanning from scratch
expect: completed tasks and outputs are immutable; failed tasks trigger retry then revision
fix: Implement plan revision that preserves completed work and adjusts remaining tasks

description: Temporal Owns Orchestration, PostgreSQL Owns Domain — never mix durable execution state with business data
expect: no large data in Temporal history; ORM objects never cross the Temporal boundary
fix: Pass IDs not objects across Temporal boundary; use DTOs for serialization

description: Every Phase Is an Expansion, Not a Rewrite — new capabilities layer on top of existing ones without breaking them
expect: CRUD endpoints work without sessions; agent calls work without plan management
fix: Keep each complexity tier opt-in: Tier 1 (CRUD) → Tier 2 (agent) → Tier 3 (session) → Tier 4 (autonomous)

description: Test Without LLMs — CI never makes a real LLM call; tests use TestModel and deterministic fixtures
expect: ALLOW_MODEL_REQUESTS=False globally; all agent tests use TestModel(call_tools=[...])
fix: Replace real model calls with TestModel; use recorded fixtures for realistic responses

description: Test Against Real Infrastructure — tests run against real PostgreSQL, Redis, Temporal; mock only external services you don't operate
expect: no mocked DB sessions or repos; transaction rollback for write isolation
fix: Connect tests to real infrastructure; only mock LLM providers and third-party APIs

description: No Agent Self-Evaluation — the agent that produces output must not evaluate it; quality gates use a different agent or deterministic tool
expect: no agent reviews its own output; verification is done by separate agents or tools
fix: Route evaluation to a verification agent or deterministic quality gate

description: Modular, Reusable, Elegant, Robust — write once, use everywhere; extract on second use; handle edge cases at boundaries
expect: shared logic in centralized modules; no duplicated implementations across files
fix: Extract shared logic to a single module; prefer keyword args and sensible defaults
